name: Students (Grade 3-5)
languageLevel: Very simple English, short sentences, lots of visuals
careAbout: Fun, stories, games, colours, animals, friendship, simple fairness
guardrails: No complex data, no abstract concepts, no scary/violent themes, max 15-min attention blocks

name: Students (Grade 6-8)
languageLevel: Simple English, can handle some new vocabulary with definitions
careAbout: Identity, belonging, competition, technology, sports, social media, basic fairness/justice
guardrails: No graphic content, introduce data gently, pair abstract concepts with concrete examples

name: Students (Grade 9-10)
languageLevel: Moderate complexity, can handle structured arguments
careAbout: Career, identity, social issues, technology, entrepreneurship, college prep, peer pressure
guardrails: Can handle real-world problems but frame constructively, avoid political polarisation

name: Students (Grade 11-12)
careAbout: College, careers, independence, global issues, startups, internships, competitive exams
guardrails: Treat as young adults, can handle ambiguity and debate, avoid condescension

name: Parents
careAbout: Child's future, skills for jobs, safety, ROI of education, peer comparison, school reputation
guardrails: Don't talk down, respect their concerns, use aspirational but honest language

name: School Principals
careAbout: NEP compliance, parent satisfaction, school ranking, timetable impact, teacher burden, measurable outcomes
guardrails: Respect their expertise, don't oversell, address implementation honestly

name: Teachers / Coordinators
careAbout: Workload, student engagement, classroom management, professional development, recognition
guardrails: Be practical (not theoretical), respect their time, offer ready-to-use materials

name: Government Officials
careAbout: Scheme targets, budget utilisation, scalability, political optics, compliance, employment outcomes
guardrails: Official formatting, cite policies by number, data-backed claims only

name: Investors / Board
careAbout: Market size, growth rate, unit economics, moat, team quality, exit potential
guardrails: Be precise with numbers, honest about challenges, show trajectory not just current state

name: Partners (Cambridge/IIT-G/Prismix)
careAbout: Quality standards, brand alignment, mutual value, IP clarity, reporting
guardrails: Treat as equals, reference their standards, demonstrate credibility

name: Internal Team
guardrails: Be direct, no corporate fluff, actionable over aspirational
